text: AI is increasingly common in modern workplaces. One major role is automation, where AI handles repetitive tasks like data entry or sorting emails, allowing employees to focus on more creative work. Many productivity tools now use AI to streamline scheduling, generate reports, or summarise meetings. Instead of replacing humans, the trend is human-AI collaboration, where people work alongside AI to make better decisions. For example, AI can analyse large datasets, while a human interprets the results. This shift means some jobs will change; routine roles may disappear, but new ones in AI management and training are emerging. Ultimately, understanding how to work with AI is becoming an essential job skill.
